name: Google Photos
short_description: A cloud photo and video service that uses machine learning to search, organize, and resurface a person's visual memories.
long_description: Launched at Google I/O 2015, Google Photos applies face, object, and scene recognition to a user's entire camera roll so people can search their memories in plain language ('beach', 'dog', a person's name) and have albums, highlights, and edits generated automatically. As a software-only platform riding on the cameras of billions of phones, it is a key example of physical AI in daily life: it does not have a body, but it perceives and interprets the physical world captured by everyday devices and acts as an ambient autobiographical memory aid. Its sensing of faces, places, and scenes also makes it a recurring touchstone in debates over visual privacy and on-by-default machine perception.

images.caption: The Google Photos app icon (2020-2025 design), a four-color pinwheel.
images.alt_text: The Google Photos logo: a pinwheel of four rounded petals in blue, red, yellow, and green arranged around a white center.

sources.title: Google Photos: A new beginning
sources.access_notes: Official launch announcement at Google I/O 2015.
